pi_items.source: Task instructions, setup_task.sh directory creation, and verifier.py logic.
WGS84 geodesic distances calculated using pyproj on the precise coordinates found in the provided ne_110m_populated_places.shp.
Used in verifier.py to bound the acceptable distance measurements.

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on a real Natural Earth shapefile containing city locations. Independent verification of the shapefile's exact coordinates yields WGS84 geodesic distances of ~5585 km (New York-London), ~7792 km (Tokyo-Sydney), and ~6890 km (Buenos Aires-Cape Town). The task's claimed ground truth values (5570, 7800, 6890) are highly accurate approximations of these true distances. The verifier accepts values within a 15% tolerance of these targets and successfully checks for the output file at /home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/distances.txt.
